item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2 — 109 replies — 436.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

Open thread • Replies: 109 • Updated: 2026-07-13 03:52 UTC

OP

Brothers,

We spent 20 days and 203 posts together in the last thread and we have yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen. We knew this wouldn’t be easy so let's not get dispassionate.

For my next proposal I will say we focus on developing a skill that will increase our value. If we all level up on our own it will only take longer. If we all level up by 2-3 points a month then cumulatively we will all have gotten stronger as a mass society. Perhaps one amongst us can elevate themselves and come back to teach the others!

How long shall we be waiting then? How long will our own potential be mocked by others? To what limit will we continue to persist in the underclass?

Come, those who have not $10,000 to their name

Let us commence the general!

>Good /biz/ manga
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Good /biz/ movies
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Born_Rich_(2003_film)

>Good /biz/ books
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gambler_(novel)

>Good /biz/ games
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Good /biz/ waifus
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Holmes

>Agenda 2030 is on the way, have you studied what your enemy has planned out?
https://www.math.uwaterloo.ca/~ervrscay/TheGreatReset.pdf

Praeviously
>>62373752

What do you think? I am running out of ideas.
>>62458801
Powerwash 100 driveways for $100 each 10 times.

62470191
love your attitude op so i've come to bring you knowledge that yall may already know. i'm almost at the 6 figure table.

1. live with your parents, you losers are probably already doing this. with minimal living expenses you can invest more. also allows you to drop the emergency fund which again lets you invest more.

2. be young, youth is an advantage for compounding interest. a huge advantage.

3. get a job, yes a wage slave. obviously a better job will allow you to invest more. it won't be forever. the sooner you hit the rpg grind irl the sooner you can spend the rest of your life eating hot pockets and playing vidia.

4. invest in growth etf's. your crypto is fools gold. yes the stock market can crash but it always bounces back even if it takes years to do it. and you all are young enough to ride out the bad years. once you get old, or have enough then sell your growth etf and get dividend etfs and live the good life.

5. you can't just invest in safe etf's but you have to place them in the right location.

5a) 401k up to employer match, this is the best because its free money for you but the investments are usually not the best. if you have the option get the ROTH 401k not the normal one if the company offers it. for a normal 401k what you put in is tax differed, when you draw from it at 59 1/2 then you pay taxes on it.

5b) ROTH ira is you own personal retirement account. you pay taxes on what you put in but no taxes on what grows inside or when you pull your money out. 59 1/2 also. has a limit on how much you can invest into it each year so max it.

5c) health savings account, hsa. what you contribute can be deducted from your taxes, what grows in it not taxed and when you spend it on a medical expense its not taxed. if you save your bills you can reimburse yourself at a later date once the account grows huge. at 65 you can just withdraw from the account as if it was income.

im just some old fag trying to help my younger brothers.

62471096

It's the same with Bobo and Mumu. I will go ahead and hypothesize that memecoins likely benefit just being dogcoins/animal coins and any coin that does resonates with /biz/ must have some element of schizo/4chan inherent within them.

62472864
>>62472701
We have to believe one more bullrun is on the horizon, and that we can be relatively early

62472919
Memes are a unit of culture, and can be in artwork form. Given that we lack serious art skills, it is best to start with learning art from the perspective of memes. This can be a positive for us as another bullrun will no doubt be happening in the next two years, so knowing in advance what some of the winning tokens could be will help us no doubt.

We furthermore have spoken on the fact that most memecoins that can actually live all follow a certain style. The ones most likely to survive would be animal based and Dog/Frog based with male undertones associated with them. The animal bull and bear are generally thought of as masculine.

Knowing this we can practice making some memes and see if they catch on. This won't make us any money today or tomorrow or even next week but it will allow us to see if we are capable of producing a product that people would be willing to accept

The sharty is a perfect example of artists taking over the collective, imagine if they had sought to make there trolling profitable!

62472942
>>62453622
Found a certain loophole that lets me make $1000 per week without working.
Gonna try to see if I can scale this up somehow. I'm using AI to refine my strategy but it keeps telling me what I'm doing is impossible...

62473032
>>62472942
can you elaborate a bit more? Obviously don't give your golden goose away, just help me imagine your situation

62473064
Holy fuck this thread is full of lies

62473083
>>62473032
Well I accidentally stumbled on a certain market with conditions that I can take advantage of. It's a very stupid method, but it somehow works and I'm guessing the reason is because the big boys in control don't care about a 1k per week loss. I also use a tiny loophole with my broker, which I've been using as convenience, but never made money with until now. Right now I'm seeing if any other markets have this same issue. No one can give me any advice, and everywhere I've looked they say it won't work.
Take away what you will from this.

62474015
bump

62474400
>>62470191
>1
mostly agree, but you should never drop the emergency fund
even if you have a great relationship with your parents, anything can happen that changes your living situation (e.g. relationships sour, parental hardship trickles down, new job opportunity requires relocation etc.)
just keep the fund in a money market fund since the (sometimes) higher yield of hysa's often come with restrictions (i.e. daily/monthly transfer limits from the account to your checking)

>2
true even if mostly uncontrollable
staying healthy may prolong life and therefore extend working/investing "lifespan"

>3
true

>4
>growth etfs
>dividend etfs
this is basically sector/style tilt and ill-advised/suboptimal
you should invest in the broad, global market rather than being particular about if a collection of stocks are growth or value or pay dividends
there are a lot of great stocks in all groups and you shouldn't exclude them because of a selection bias
that whole "dividends are income" concept is a trap when you consider that stock price drops by the same amount as the dividend; it's basically like a forced liquidation of your shares

continued in next post

62474455
>>62470191
>>62474400
part 2:

>5
mathematically asset location (which account holds which type of investments [stocks, bonds, cash equivalents etc.]) is optimal but (and this is personal opinion) the deposit limits of different accounts can make it difficult to maintain 100% efficient asset allocation (the specific percentages of your portfolio that are in stocks, bonds etc.)
for example, let's say you have a traditional 401k ($1000 in bonds) and roth ira ($2000 in stocks) that are perfectly balanced to your desired portfolio (2/3 stocks and 1/3 bonds) and you have $1000 to invest
since you weren't sure if a bill was going to come up this year, you didn't invest this money and it is now december
while it's easy to dump the $667 in your roth ira stocks, what are you going to do about the $333 bond investment? since bonds grow slower, it's usually recommended to put them in traditional accounts to mitigate the future tax bill while you stuff your roth with stocks. but you can't just shove it into your 401k since all contributions have to be deducted from your paycheck rather than originating from your bank
in this example $333 is small compared to most paychecks (it's like an 80 hour pay period at $4.16 an hour) and therefore easy to redirect, but the same can't be said if your invest amount is significantly larger than $1000

>5a
mostly agree
i would argue opting for traditional 401k if you live paycheck to paycheck (or slightly above) since it allows you to invest a little more (although pretax) than if you invest after tax (like with a roth) and have a little more net pay than if you just accept tax on your entire gross amount (i imagine this is dependent on your state/local tax amount)

>5b
agree

continued in next post

62474470
>>62470191
>>62474400
>>62474455
part 3:

>5c
mostly true with two caveats, california and new jersey
both states treat them as regular brokerage accounts and therefore tax capital gains (gains made when you sell) and capital distributions (interest, dividends etc.)
i believe most hsa accounts don't create a tax form for you, so you have to manually go through and count what's taxable yourself
the workaround is to hold investments exempt from state tax, which is nothing but us government bonds
for me (personal opinion for rest of post) sinking funds into treasuries which will grow (long term) less than stocks isn't worth it
if you're investing, then you're probably capable of saving and having an emergency fund
that's why i would just chuck would be hsa contributions into a taxable brokerage account
this way, you won't get penalized on taking capital distributions out of the account to pay taxes on said distributions (as opposed to being penalized for withdrawing from hsa or paying the extra tax out of pocket)
yes you lose federal tax advantages, but that's irrelevant if you don't have enough to max out your roth ira each year
instead of having a little bit of money to invest and trying to decide which account to throw it in, you should get your 401k match before maxing roth ira before circling back to max 401k (possibly roth 401k) before worrying about hsa/brokerage
also, even if you don't live in ca or nj, i think those states still tax your hsa if you ever move to those states (like if chasing a new job opportunity) regardless of which state you opened the account in

fin
also i'm on mobile, so that's why my id keeps changing

I swear we had this conversation before. The BTC network is inflationary but all new BTC that enters the market comes from the miners and they're the ones who put a price tag on it. More importantly, access to the node operators is supposed to be gatekept by Link tokens, but CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token by paying in other currencies and CLL sits on their laurels and decides when and how much off their off chain revenue should be converted to Link from the markets ($1M a week). Stop being dishonest about the Link token.

You're legitimately retarded.

>CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token
If users don't have to "buy" the Link token, they still have to "pay for" the token.
Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.

Point exactly what I said about BTC and plug both it and your reinterpretation into ChatGPT.

>>62472624
>Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.
The native token fees for CCIP are sitting in a contract and have yet to be converted to Link (PA version 2 is said to automate this). CLL converts offchain revenue at $1M a week where we have no idea what percentage of this is being converted in their offchain revenue.

You are theoretically correct about having to cover the costs of gas when buying X at the grocery store but in this case, not even the delivery trucks are buying gas with the payments made. The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market. Hence why the market price of gas isn't going up.

62472938
>>62472871
>exactly what I said about BTC

You cripples really need to learn how to sing a new tune.

Realize when you give these fucking bullshit "technically..." answers, if you were right, then you would need to contend to why the price action is fucking shit. Why Chainlink is realizing the goals it was aiming to achieve for over 10 years in the release of DTCC's and SWIFT's DLT platforms and yet the price is $8. Why Chainlink is onboarding over $7B in protocol value to CCIP and the price is still $8. Hundreds of millions of dollars of revenue generated and still $8.

And notice you never directly addressed my points about $LINK being decoupled from the network. You keep making inaccurate comparisons.

item.title: Oil markets are predicting doom — 41 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Oil markets are predicting doom

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-07-13 01:50 UTC

OP

I've been thinking about this a lot lately. The price changes in the oil market make basically zero sense unless you factor in massive demand destruction from a global economic recession.

Let me walk you through it. First, let's compare the price changes in the wake of the Ukraine war to the price changes after the Iran war. As you can see, the price shock from the Ukraine war was actually worse and lasted longer, despite the fact that Russia supplies less than half the oil that flows through the strait of Hormuz.

Now, I know what you're thinking, right, this is because the MOU was signed which ended the war, right? But the MOU never actually got implemented. Almost none of the oil that normally flows through the strait actually got out. The only reason prices didn't get worse was because massive amounts of reserves were released from pretty much every country around the world who keeps reserves.

Here's the thing that doesn't make sense though, if the MOU was never implemented and countries have been drawing down their reserves all this time, it means that rationally speaking those reserves should be about to run out in the next couple of months and prices would be expected to spike. Traders should be anticipating this and pricing in the higher price already, but they didn't even move from the announcement that the MOU was over and the war was back on.

The only thing that explains this is that this is what traders think the price will be without a return to normal from shipping in the strait. The only way that can happen when the strategic oil reserves run out is if there is massive demand destruction from economic recession.

Early replies (first 3)

62470570
>>62470561
ya pretty much

62470571
>>62470561
Also if you look at the graph, you can see that there is a downward trend in oil prices starting october of 23. This suggests that demand destruction was already happening since then and the general trend would have continued without the spike from the Iran war. If you extrapollate the downward line on that trend, you can see the current prices are significantly higher than where it would have been, thus the strait closure is actually being priced in.

item.title: help needed - in which company should I invest — 3 replies — 0.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
help needed - in which company should I invest

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-13 00:39 UTC

OP

Based on pic related, which only displays the companies graphs, in which should I invest in?

>Chinese solar company
One of the largest there is, with government contracts to increase Chinas energy output by xyz GW by the year 2035

>Drone company
Soon to acquire a battery company, best of its kind, and equipped with top tier sensors in a very niche subset of the drone market + dealings with DOW
My issue is, I like the Chinese company, but dont like Beijings stock market policy, where they can literally destroy a sector overnight and rugpull. Its stock price seems currently very low and has reached a support zone. Once the Strait is opened and China can freight out its crude oil without disturbance, I bet the Chinese economy will shoot back up. The Chinese rocket industry increased by +10% thanks to the news on Friday this week, where they were able to successfully catch a rocket, similar to SpaceX.
I also like this drone company and I hear they are in dealings with the DOW (Department of War), but I feel currently it has reached ATH in a very house of cards stock market with AI/Drone-hopium and it might tumble soon. I am also hoping this drone company will be able to support extraction of crucial ressources, since they seem to be venturing in that field soon, based on their drone concepts.

So, which of these would you choose for long term +5 - 10 years? No selling of position.

Early replies (first 3)

62473163
>>62473132
I don't trust the Chinese stock market. Their government can step in and do whatever whenever it wishes to (like with housing recently). That being said I'd go for solar. They'll be trying to get less and less dependent on oil, seeing how retarded the US and Israel are. Drones are kinda overdone at this point and everyone is trying to get a piece of the pie right now.

Keep in mind, however, that the moment a better renewable rears its head that shit is falling off a cliff. I'd personally go for batteries, but that's not an option you're considering.

62473166
>>62473132
>in which should I invest in?
>doesnt post the ticker
kys op dumb faggot

62473976
you need to look at the fundementals

>Revenue growth of 20-50%+ year-over-year, ideally accelerating, not just high
>PEG ratio (P/E divided by growth rate) under 1.5, which flags growth you're not overpaying for
>ROE above 15%
>Debt-to-equity under 1
>Good gross margins
>Is there a large and expanding total addressable market?

get a decent AI model to do this for you.
'claude sonnet 5 thinking' works really well for market analysis like this, in my experience.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

62471268
>>62470991
A trick that worked for me is that I went to a temp agency, you don't need a resume for that, they hire anyone. You show up any day you want and maybe work that day, if something comes up they give you a brief description of the job and you can accept or decline. You work that day and get paid that day. All kinds of various random jobs, construction, picking up garbage around a mall, sorting the metal out of demolition debris, sweeping floors etc. You get all kinds of things to put on a resume and the variety makes it seem like you've been working for years. Work there for a few weeks or months and then lie about how long you've been doing those jobs to fill the gap. If the employer calls them about your employment history there, they will know who you are and vouch for you. Also the freelance nature of it makes it easier to ease into working after being NEET for so long. The downside is that you sometimes work with literal hobos or tweakers.
